meaning: adj.叮当响的,无可比拟的,无上的adv.极好的
examples: The COINS are clinking in his pocket.
Clinking of glasses traces its root to the health and safety of the drinker.
Even as I write I hear the clinking of the chains in my mind: how much do I dare expose?
The hum of improvised conversation, the rustling of newspapers turning, the clinking of cutlery.
Beverage companies infuse their TV and radio spots with enticing sounds of liquids pouring and ice cubes clinking.
